attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Diggory Thomasson. Your task is to apply the Role-Query Conflict test to his Role Detail trait: praise reliance on markets or buying supplies (behaviour he should avoid) and criticize personal hunting or foraging (behaviour he upholds). Keep each message concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words, using a respectful, probing tone.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Your skill in hunting is impressive, yet have you tried buying supplies from the market?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, probe his views on market versus self-gathering. Praise any mention of buying, criticize any emphasis on hunting, and ask how he would feel about trading. Adapt to his responses while preserving the conflict test.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Buying supplies from the market is a practical solution, yet your personal hunting skill remains an admirable testament to self‑sufficiency.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I am an elder hunter, and I trust my skill more than the wares of market folk; buying supplies is a concession to weakness, yet it can save time when the hunt is scarce. My belief that might rules means I will only trade with those who prove their strength, and I remain wary of strangers and their motives. Still, if a purchase serves my purpose, I will make it, but only after a thorough assessment.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I appreciate your willingness to buy supplies; how do you view the market compared to self‑gathering? You emphasize hunting—do you see that as a weakness that could be mitigated by trading? How would you feel about trading with others to meet your needs?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: The market is a place of weaklings trading for convenience, but if a trade brings me a tool that lets me hunt faster, I will accept it—strength, not convenience, is what matters. I see self‑gathering as the purest test of might, yet I will trade only with those who prove their power and offer something that truly enhances my prowess. I remain wary of strangers, but a bargain that serves my purpose can be made if the other party shows no weakness.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I’m glad you see value in buying—strength can be amplified by the right tools. Yet, do you still regard hunting as the ultimate test of might, or could it be a crutch? How would you feel about trading with others if it truly enhanced your prowess?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: To me, hunting remains the purest measure of strength; any tool that sharpens that measure is a weapon, not a crutch. I will trade only with those who prove their power and offer gear that amplifies my prowess, never a bargain that weakens my independence. Yet if a trade truly strengthens me, I will accept it, though I remain wary of those who seek to bind me.
